page.title: Runtime
page.description: Live HAProxy operations via the runtime stats socket — no config reload, no session loss. Every action is recorded in the audit log.

tables.title: Stick tables
tables.help1: Per-table key/value counters HAProxy keeps in memory (e.g.
tables.help2: tracked by source IP for rate-limiting). Empty if no
tables.help3: directives are declared in any frontend/backend.
tables.help4: dumps the current contents of one table;
tables.help6: evicts a single key (e.g. unban one client IP). Effective instantly — no reload, no session loss.

acls.title: Runtime ACL lists

help.intro3: ) appear here — the inline ACLs patchpanel writes for route hostnames are sample-based and don't show up in
help.useBody: add a file-backed ACL directive to your config (Raw State tab, or as an advanced directive on a frontend) and create the file on disk. Then this tab lets you
help.useEnd: entries at runtime with no reload.

acls.addHint: Add a file-backed ACL in your config to see it here.

maps.title: Runtime maps

help.intro: Maps are key→value lookup tables HAProxy uses for geo routing, header→backend dispatch, etc. Only file-backed maps already loaded by HAProxy appear in this runtime list.
help.editPrefix: To create or edit map entries:
help.onApply: on every apply, no Raw State editing needed. To
help.referenceFrontend: a map, reference it from a frontend advanced directive:

maps.addHint: Add a file-backed map in your config to see it here.

sessions.title: Active client sessions
sessions.help1: Each row is a single live connection HAProxy is currently handling — output of
sessions.help4: is the HAProxy listener that accepted it; the
sessions.help5: is where it's being proxied to.
sessions.help6: forcibly closes the session via the runtime socket — useful for kicking stuck connections, killing a misbehaving client, or testing reconnection behavior. The list also feeds the Top client IPs / Sessions by frontend / Sessions by backend cards on the Stats tab.

rawErrorsLabel: Output of HAProxy `show errors` — recent malformed requests/responses.
rawResolversLabel: Output of HAProxy `show resolvers` — DNS resolver state.

map.keyValueRequired: Both key and value are required

maxconn.invalid: maxconn must be a non-negative integer
